channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Sonnet 5
item.description: Jul 7, 07:37 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 7, 06:58 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Simplexity Robotics: 11-Month-Old Embodied AI Startup Ships 100 Robots to Production Lines
item.description: Simplexity Robotics, an embodied AI robotics startup founded less than a year ago, announced the first batch delivery of 100 units of its all-scenario robot i7 Pro, ...

item.title: BeyondTrust Patches Critical Auth Bypass Flaws in Remote Support and PRA
item.description: BeyondTrust has released updates to address two critical security flaws affecting Remote Support (RS) and Privileged Remote Access (PRA) products that, if successfully exploited, could allow unauthenticated attackers to take control of susceptible devices. The vulnerabilities are listed below -...

item.title: Wiener Intelligence Becomes First Chinese Data Generation Company Published in Nature
item.description: On May 28, 2026, Nature Communications published a paper titled "Multimodal deep learning model for AI-based functional prognostic risk stratification in patien...

item.title: [AINews] The Field Guide to Fable
item.description: a quiet day lets us digest the world's most significant model launch... to date.

item.title: Hangzhou's Diagens Launches iMedLoop, Building a Full-Chain Medical AI Ecosystem
item.description: On July 4, Diagens held the "Medical AI Ecosystem Innovation Symposium and iMedLoop Global Medical Imaging Data Platform Launch" at the Diaoyutai State Gu...

item.title: iFLYTEK-Embodied-Omni Technical Report
item.description: arXiv:2607.02542v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: General-purpose embodied agents must understand multimodal instructions, anticipate how their environment will evolve, and produce precise control actions over extended horizons. Existing approaches typically specialize in visual-language reasoning,...

item.title: Internal Pluralism and the Limits of Pairwise Comparisons
item.description: arXiv:2607.02672v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Local pairwise comparisons are a standard tool for learning how people want decision rules to work, e.g., in participatory design or alignment. However, their use builds in two strong assumptions: that local comparisons are sufficient evidence about...

item.title: ASK in the Dark: Uncertainty-Gated LLM Assistance under Partial Observability
item.description: arXiv:2607.02686v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning agents operating under partial observability must act on incomplete information, making them natural candidates for guidance from small language models (SLMs) that carry broad reasoning priors. Yet integrating SLM guidance into...

item.title: Automated Data Readiness for Scientific AI
item.description: arXiv:2607.02771v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Leadership computing facilities steward large-scale scientific datasets that routinely require substantial transformation before serving as AI training data. However, no existing framework fully unifies automated transformation, readiness assessment,...

item.title: SwarmResearch: Orchestrating Coding Agents for Open-Ended Discovery
item.description: arXiv:2607.02807v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-running coding agents such as autoresearch can persistently discover optimizations for open-ended problems. However, they tend to converge onto a single high-level approach, then proceed with low-level edits while missing other superior...

item.title: Auditing the Audit: Five Failure Modes in Benchmark-Validity Audits
item.description: arXiv:2607.02586v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Governance frameworks ask AI providers and auditors for documented evaluation evidence, and perturbation-based construct-validity audits are a common form of that evidence. We argue the audits are themselves fragile: their conclusions can be silently...

item.title: Evaluating Time Series Foundation Models for Electricity Price Forecasting: Contamination Risk, Distributional Shifts, and Covariate Dependence
item.description: arXiv:2607.02623v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time series foundation models (TSFMs) have shown strong zero-shot forecasting performance, but their generalization in covariate-driven, non-stationary settings is underexplored. Electricity price forecasting (EPF) presents a challenging testbed due...

item.title: QuantFlow: A Federated Mamba-Based Post-Transformer Foundation Model for Time-Series Forecasting
item.description: arXiv:2607.02632v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Time-series forecasting supports decisions in finance, en-ergy, transportation, public health, and industrial monitoring. Recent foundation models improve transfer across forecast-ing tasks, but many depend on centralized data and Trans-former...

item.title: GRAFT: Grafted Reference Audio for Fine-grained Pronunciation in Zero-shot Text-to-Speech
item.description: arXiv:2607.02633v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present GRAFT, a per-word pronunciation conditioning mechanism for text-to-speech neural codec language modeling. Existing systems reach high intelligibility and naturalness but inherit the ambiguity of text and mispronounce rare proper nouns,...

item.title: Federated Learning for Object Detection: Enabling Collaborative Drone Learning Without Centralizing Data
item.description: arXiv:2607.02636v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Object detection is a fundamental capability for AI-driven perception in safety-critical drone and edge-vision systems, including disaster response, operational security environments, infrastructure monitoring and defense applications. Robust model...

item.title: Improving LLMs via Validator-to-Generator Alignment
item.description: arXiv:2607.02668v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models are inconsistent: varying prompts or including unrelated information can lead to unexpected changes in model outputs. The generator-validator (G-V) gap is one manifestation of this phenomenon, where LLMs generate responses that...

item.title: Echoes of Unrest: A Multimodal NLP Framework for Early Warning of Fake News and Violence-Driven Mob Activity
item.description: arXiv:2607.02734v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Rapid growth in social media has transformed global communication by enabling fast information exchange, but it has also accelerated the spread of misinformation. Fake news, manipulated content, and provocative narratives are increasingly linked to...

item.title: Reinforcement Learning for Data-Efficient Code-Switched ASR
item.description: arXiv:2607.02757v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Audio-language models can be prompted for code-switched speech, but their decoding is not optimized for code-switching and often fails at language boundaries. We propose a practical reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards recipe for...

item.title: LuxSQA: Ask Me in Luxembourgish with TTS-Augmented Spoken Question Answering
item.description: arXiv:2607.02763v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Spoken Question Answering (SQA) remains largely focused on high-resource languages and carefully recorded speech, limiting the reach of speech-LLM methods in low-resource settings. This paper investigates whether text-to-speech (TTS) can provide...

item.title: Gemma 4 Technical Report
item.description: arXiv:2607.02770v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Gemma 4, a new generation of open-weight, natively multimodal language models in the Gemma model family. Designed to advance compute efficiency and reasoning, the Gemma 4 model suite features dense and Mixture-of-Experts architectures,...

item.title: AI Browser's $10 Billion Prize: Why Nobody Has Won It Yet
item.description: Despite dozens of attempts from startups and tech giants alike, AI-powered browsers have failed to displace Chrome as users' default gateway to the web.

item.title: World's First Neurodynamic Chip Matches Brain Speed: Peking University Breakthrough in Science
item.description: PKU team unveils the world's first phase-change memristor neural dynamical system chip, achieving 2.12ms step latency and up to 478x speedup over NVIDIA A100 for brain modeling.

item.title: Claude Tag seeing elevated GitHub operation failures
item.description: Jul 6, 21:49 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating elevated error rates on calls made Claude Tag. Our team is actively working to identify the root cause. You may experience errors such as GitHub operations denied.

item.title: Durable Objects Increased Error Rate in ENAM
item.description: Jul 7, 00:42 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 6, 23:41 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are currently monitoring the results. Jul 6, 22:56 UTC Identified - Cloudflare has identified issue causing an increase of errors for Durable Objects and downstream services...

item.title: LeRobot v0.6.0: Imagine, Evaluate, Improve
Elevated errors on Claude.ai
item.description: Jul 6, 20:05 UTC Resolved - This issue has been resolved. Jul 6, 19:46 UTC Monitoring - We have applied a fix, and at this time are seeing recovery of Claude.ai and related services such as OAuth log-in for Claude Code. Jul 6, 19:28 UTC Update - We have identified and are working to resolve an...

item.title: Blogspot-Hosted Payloads Delivered in ‘Veil#Drop’ Attacks
item.description: Securonix says the sophisticated framework abuses compromised websites, Blogspot, PowerShell, and fileless techniques to evade detection and deploy the PureLog information stealer. The post Blogspot-Hosted Payloads Delivered in ‘Veil#Drop’ Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Iran-Linked Hackers Use New Cavern C2 Framework to Target Israeli Organizations
item.description: An Iranian hacking group affiliated with Iran's Ministry of Intelligence and Security (MOIS) has been wielding a previously undocumented modular command-and-control (C2) framework dubbed Cavern (aka Cav3rn) targeting Israeli organizations. The activity, which has primarily singled out IT providers...

item.title: Your family’s $300 stake in OpenAI
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. OpenAI CEO Sam Altman’s oft-discussed promise that Americans will share in the wealth AI creates was in the news again last week. On Thursday, the Financial...

item.title: 16-Year-Old Linux KVM Flaw Lets Guest VMs Escape to Host on Intel and AMD x86 Systems
item.description: A use-after-free bug in Linux's KVM hypervisor can be triggered from a guest virtual machine to corrupt the shadow-page state of the host kernel that runs it. Dubbed 'Januscape' and tracked as CVE-2026-53359, the flaw sits in the shadow MMU code that KVM shares across both Intel and AMD. The...

item.title: Threat Actors Probe Gitea Docker Flaw CVE-2026-20896 13 Days After Disclosure
item.description: Threat actors have been observed attempting to exploit a recently patched critical security flaw in Gitea Docker images, according to Sysdig. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-20896 (CVSS score: 9.8), a vulnerability that stems from the DevOps platform trusting the "X-WEBAUTH-USER" header...

item.title: PRX Part 4: Our Data Strategy
The Shift Toward Business-Aligned Risk Management
item.description: Moving from isolated, technical data to a continuous risk lifecycle can help organizations align security controls with actual business consequences. The post The Shift Toward Business-Aligned Risk Management appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Armored Likho APT Targeting Government, Electric Power Entities
item.description: The threat actor uses modular RATs and information stealers in financially motivated and cyber espionage campaigns. The post Armored Likho APT Targeting Government, Electric Power Entities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: North Korean Hackers Target Open Source Developers in Supply Chain Attacks
item.description: The PolinRider campaign has compromised more than 100 legitimate open source packages and repositories to deliver a backdoor and information stealer to developers. The post North Korean Hackers Target Open Source Developers in Supply Chain Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Proxy Botnets, Browser Ransomware, AI Agent Tricks, Fake PoC Malware and More
item.description: A streaming box should not need a threat model. Neither should a username field, a demo repo, a reset flow, or a browser permission prompt. That is the irritating part this week: the risky pieces were ordinary. Home devices became a routing cover. Clean code pulled dirt from a dependency. Identity...

item.title: The Download: South Korea’s hottest bachelors, and advancing eye transplants
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. South Korea’s hottest new bachelors are chip workers Baek, a 35-year-old manager at the South Korean semiconductor titan SK Hynix, was enrolled in a...

item.title: Proof-of-Concept Exploit Released for Linux ‘Bad Epoll’ Root Access Vulnerability
item.description: Organizations are urged to patch after proof-of-concept code makes the Linux root escalation flaw easier to exploit. The post Proof-of-Concept Exploit Released for Linux ‘Bad Epoll’ Root Access Vulnerability appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: South Korea’s hottest new bachelors are chip workers
item.description: Baek, a 35-year-old manager at the South Korean semiconductor titan SK Hynix, was enrolled in Sunoo, a matchmaking company based in Seoul, a year ago. In a move typical of anxious South Korean parents, his mother signed him up, hoping to find a good wife for her son. Lately, says Baek (who asked to...

item.title: Zero Trust Dashboard onboarding issues
item.description: Jul 6, 08:12 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue where some users are currently unable to complete onboarding into Cloudflare Zero Trust or manage Access applications. We are actively working on a resolution.

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Haiku 4.5
item.description: Jul 6, 07:36 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: 🤗 Kernels: Major Updates
Elevated number of errors in Munich, Germany
item.description: Jul 5, 05:35 UTC Resolved - Between 05:07-05:19 UTC customers reaching Munich may have experienced an elevated number of 4XX or 5XX errors. The issue is now resolved

item.title: Cloudflare Vectorize Issues
item.description: Jul 4, 22:47 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 4, 21:25 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with the Vectorize service. Customers may receive an "Internal Server Error" (HTTP 500) response when sending requests to a Vectorize index that has been deleted, instead...

item.title: New users unable to deploy Workers
item.description: Jul 4, 15:32 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 4, 15:26 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 4, 14:38 UTC Identified - Cloudflare has identified an issue where new users cannot deploy Workers in the dashboard. We are working to...

item.title: Elevated errors across many models
item.description: Jul 4, 00:20 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jul 4, 00:19 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jul 3, 23:42 UTC Investigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: A device that revives eyeballs from dead donors could make eye transplants possible
item.description: It’s not easy to transplant a whole human eye. The surgery is difficult. And the eyes themselves start to degenerate as soon as they’ve left the body. When surgeons attempted it a few years ago, the newly-transplanted eye wasn’t able to see. But researchers believe they might have a solution: a...

item.title: Google DeepMind and A24 announce first-of-its-kind research partnership
The Download: a smoking “endgame” and a new Elizabeth Bear story
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. The UK’s generational tobacco ban might not work. I’m supporting it anyway. —Jessica Hamzelou As the parent of two little girls, I often think about...
